title: AxonField Supportware
preview:
Summary: mid-market SaaS for customer support teams. Pushing knowledge-base RAG into support UI (in-app answer suggestions + agent assist). Primary asks: high QPS (sustained ~200-300 qps), predictable per-query cost, and sub-150ms 95th latency for top-k rerank flow. Currently prototyping with third-party vector store + OpenAI embeddings; want single vendor for hosted embedding + rerank to simplify SLAs and lower egress/cost.

Key customer constraints (from calls):
- Index size: ~6-12M doc chunks (moderate scale).
- Query pattern: short queries, many concurrent users, spikes during support events.
- Cost sensitivity: wants per-query model to be ~<$0.006 median to justify replacing current provider.
- Security: SSO (SAML), SOC2 required, audit logs + configurable retention. Data residency for EU customers (subset of customers).

Quote from Head of ML: "If we can get reliable 95th p95 latency under 150ms and cost predictability we can move 80% of production traffic in Q3."

Why Redwood: likes unified runtime + routing for fallback during spikes. Interested in Redwood Optimize recommendations for batching + quantization to hit cost targets. Prefers hosted API to avoid infra ops for now.

title: Clinicentrix Health Search
preview:
Summary: Clinicentrix is building an enterprise-grade clinical document search used by 200+ hospital clinicians. Primary focus: large-scale embeddings + cost-aware reranking pipeline for PHI-protected EHR notes.

Recent activity:
- 2026-01-15: Intro call (Ava) — high-level product fit; they indexed a 30M note prototype.
- 2026-01-22: Deep-dive with SE (Mateo) — architecture constraints, ingestion cadence, delta indexing. Fireflies: ff:call_987654321.
- 2026-02-18: Security sync with InfoSec (Rita joined) — SSO, KMS, audit retention discussed; asked for SOC2 evidence and network topology.
- 2026-03-03: Cost model review; asked for POC SOW and expected token/embedding pricing for 120M doc index; we sent initial estimate (drive:/Sales/Deals/Clinicentrix/2026/Clinicentrix_Redwood_POC_Deck_v3.pdf).

Technical requirements / asks (customer language fragments):
- "Need P95 search latency <200ms for single-clinic queries; multi-site aggregate queries can be higher."
- Index size target: ~120M clinical notes (de-identified + PHI flagged for access controls).
- Throughput goal: sustained 150 QPS peak for search surface, burst to 500 QPS during morning rounds.
- Approach preferred: two-stage retrieval — sparse/pooled doc filter -> bi-encoder embeddings -> lightweight re-rank on top-50 -> cross-encoder rerank only for top-5 when allowed. Cost sensitivity: cross-encoder rerank on-host preferred to avoid hosted per-token costs.
- Embedding model: prefer open/traceable model; will evaluate Redwood-verified candidate models. Must measure semantic drift vs. clinical queries.
- Ingest: near real-time EHR stream, dedupe, redaction pipeline before indexing; want connectors run in VPC or on-prem agent.

Security / compliance specifics:
- PHI: must not leave customer-controlled enclave for protected fields; they want split-processing pattern (embeddings hashed in VPC, identifiable PHI stored in encrypted columns).
- Audit: full access logs shipped to their SIEM, 1y retention for audit trails + tamper-evident hashes.
- Key management: HSM/KMS integration mandatory for production.
- Data residency: US for majority, but pilot includes EU hospital (data residency question).

POC success criteria:
- Index 5M clinical notes into a private Redwood Dedicated pool in VPC with P95 <200ms at 150 QPS using bi-encoder + top-50 rerank.
- Demonstrate audit logging (immutable events exported) and KMS-encrypted keys for models.
- Cost projection for 120M index with expected 2.5M queries/month and 20% cross-encoder rerank traffic.

Open questions (sales/tech):
- Which Redwood model variants can be hosted in Dedicated with HSM-backed keys?
- Can automatic fallback be enforced to private model variant when capacity in hosted tier is low?
- Exact SLA for multi-region failover (we need an architecture doc).

Stated blockers / risks:
- Legal needs explicit PHI data flow diagram before POC kickoff.
- Networking team requires a 3-week maintenance window to enable VPC peering.
- Cost sensitivity: customer requests a discounted pilot rate and strict per-query budget cap.

Next steps (short):
- Book architecture + security workshop (internal invite: Mateo, Rita, Ava + Clinicentrix infra and InfoSec) — target week of 2026-03-15.
- Deliver POC SOW + line-item cost model by 2026-03-12 (action: Ava).
- Provide network topology template + sample data-flow diagram for PHI handling (action: Mateo).

title: Archway VPC Integrations Ltd.
preview:
Prospect: regional fintech (payments + risk). Very strict data residency requirements (primary in DE + FR). Primary ask: Redwood Private deployed into Archway-managed VPC with no public egress, direct connectivity (AWS PrivateLink or peering/TGW), and HSM-backed KMS for key material.

Network specifics captured during workshop: prefer PrivateLink for cross-account access but networking team concerned about endpoints per AZ and cost at scale. Alternative: VPC peering via Transit Gateway with strict route table controls. Must support IP allowlists, per-subnet firewall rules, and ability to lock down egress to a small set of controlled NAT/egress proxies.

Security review status: questionnaire 70% complete. Pending items: HSM/KMS key custody model confirmation (customer-managed CMKs vs Redwood-managed), audit_log retention window (they require 7 years for some flows), and proof of no model weights leaving the private environment. Archway wants explicit guarantee that model compute and KV caches remain in-customer VPC for Private deployment.

POC notes: running embeddings rerank pipeline against sanitized transaction logs; target p50 latency for reranking < 120ms, p95 < 350ms, throughput ~250 req/s for peak windows. Cost sensitivity: mid-high (willing to pay for guaranteed throughput and residency). Preferred model family: quantized open models (Llama-derivatives or similar) hosted inside VPC, with automated fallback to smaller local model variant if capacity constrained.

Quotes / paraphrases: "We cannot have any customer PII leave EU perimeter"; "Need technical doc for network firewall rules and exact egress CIDRs by 2026-03-15".

Operational asks: integration with Archway KMS/HSM (YubiHSM or cloud HSM), centralized audit logs shipped to Archway SIEM (syslog/S3), optional long-term retention (7 years) for selected audit events, and SSO via SAML with JIT provisioning.

Next practical steps: provide network runbook (PrivateLink vs peering pros/cons + cost estimate), KMS/HSM integration pattern docs, sample firewall/iptables templates, and updated SOW reflecting a Private deployment with 3 AZ redundancy. AE note: push for security review sign-off in next 2 weeks to keep April close.

title: Cerulean Telecom Systems
preview:
Summary: Large North American telco evaluating Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem) with air-gapped variant for NOC and central office deployments. Heavy emphasis on attestable supply chain (SBOM), FIPS/KMS integration, and streaming audit logs into Splunk.

Recent interactions: 
- 2026-03-02 call (ff_2026_0302_cerulean_call): demo of on-prem control plane; requested offline model upgrade workflow.
- 2026-02-19 security deep-dive: InfoSec requested proof-of-concepts for key management (HSM integration) and data flow diagrams. Gmail thread: thread-GM-cerulean-20260219-01.
- 2025-12-10 intro meeting with CTO and Head of Network Automation; showed latency numbers and dedicated GPU sizing.

Key requirements (from notes / quotes):
- "Cannot allow customer-PII or CPE telemetry to leave country boundary" => must support data residency and local-only inference.
- Air-gapped upgrades: signed model bundle delivery via SFTP/hard media; verified signatures on install.
- KMS/HSM: integrate with client on-prem HSM (Luna/AWS CloudHSM proxy) for envelope keys; support FIPS 140-2 validated modules.
- Audit logging: streaming token-level audit trail to Splunk, retention 7 years, immutable logs; SOC2/ISO path required.
- Network constraints: NOC has strict change windows (SLA for deploys); need deployment automation that supports maintenance windows and rollback.
- Performance: sub-30ms tail P99 for short-chat flows (local NOC inference), batch embedding throughput for nightly indexing (100k docs/hr).

POC scope discussed:
- On-prem control plane in customer VPC with private data plane inside their NOC.
- Air-gapped model update process: sign->hand-off->local-verify.
- KMS/HSM integration demo and attestation documents.
- Live audit log export to Splunk ingestion endpoint.

Open items / blockers: 
- InfoSec SRA outstanding: need SBOM, dependency vulnerability report, and installation checklist.
- Procurement: hardware lead time estimate required (GPU SKU and storage).
- Legal: finalize addendum for data residency and on-prem license terms.

Action items (AE/SE):
- Marcus to produce HSM integration runbook + sample config (due 2026-03-12) -> DONE (sent).
- Priya to coordinate procurement template with pricing (in progress).
- Send air-gapped deployment playbook + BOM (next_step).

Steno / CRM fragments: 
- "Client wants attestable SBOM + deterministic model bundles"
- "If Redwood can run in dark site NOC with the HSM integration we win"
- "Ask: can we support FIPS validated HSM modules in Private offering?"

References: drive://cerulean/security_faq_v2.pdf, drive://cerulean/vpc_onprem_install_runbook_v1.pptx, INFRA-4127, SEC-2089.

Contact list: 
- Technical buyer: Marie Kline, Head of Network Automation (marie.kline@cerulean-telecom.com)
- Procurement: Omar Reyes (omar.reyes@cerulean-telecom.com)
- InfoSec reviewer: Daniel Park (daniel.park@cerulean-telecom.com)

Likelihood / sentiment: High intent for private on-prem contract if InfoSec SRA closes; budget earmarked Q2 2026.

Short next notes to push: schedule 2-day onsite install window option; provide final BOM and signed model bundle procedure.

title: Catalyst Pylon Search
preview:
Summary: mid-market product analytics SaaS evaluating Redwood for embeddings + semantic search. Started with Hosted API (fast test), moving to Dedicated for predictable throughput and lower unit cost. Team: CTO (Anya Venkatar), Head of Search Eng (Caleb Moore), Data Eng lead (Rina Patel).

POC scope and ask: 2-week hosted-API POC to validate embedding quality + ingestion friction. Follow-on Dedicated trial to run throughput tests and pricing bake. Key metrics: embedding ingestion throughput (batch and streaming), end-to-end query latency (target <80ms for top-k retrieval), nightly batch job ingest 1M rows within a 3-hour window, cost per 1M embeddings. Vector DB integration must support hybrid search and similarity+filtering by metadata.

Tech notes from calls:
- Currently using Postgres + pgvector for small workloads; testing Pinecone demo for latency. Want to avoid re-architecting if Redwood can support Postgres-compatible workflows or provide clear connectors.
- Preference for open models where possible (Llama/Falcon family) with recommended quantized variants. Interested in Redwood Optimize suggestions for batching and KV caching.
- Data residency: keep indexed metadata in us-east-1, must support KMS-managed keys for PII fields.
- Security questionnaire submitted 2026-02-20 (see drive). SOC2 required, plus SSO/SAML and audit logs for admin actions.

Commercial/ops:
- Price sensitivity: moderate — willing to commit to Dedicated if unit economics show 25-40% savings vs hosted at target throughput.
- Procurement window: May 2026. Forecast to close Apr 2026 if Dedicated pricing aligns.

Quotes and shorthand from meetings:
- "Need a sample night ingest run with 1M vectors — if that fails we can't commit" — Caleb M.
- "Latency for discovery queries needs to be consistently under 100ms at 99p" — Anya V.

Action items on Redwood side:
- Provide Dedicated pricing tiers with committed throughput and burst policy examples.
- Run parallel throughput test on Hosted API: 200 qps sustained for embedding calls + 1M batch ingest simulation (link test harness).
- Confirm connectors for pgvector export/import and recommended vector DB partners for long-term ops.

title: Boltweave Real-Time Systems
preview:
- Company: fleet-scale mobility platform (ride-hail + logistics). Very cost sensitive at scale; forecasting heavy peak windows (08:00–10:00, 17:00–19:00 local).
- Primary workloads: conversational in-car assistant (short multi-turn chat), route re-ranking for ETA/traffic, and rapid summarization of driver reports.
- Latency targets: p50 < 30ms, p95 <= 120ms end-to-end for chat assistant (edge device + service). Throughput target: sustained 4k–6k req/s during peak, spiky bursts up to 12k req/s.
- Current stack: self-hosted on mixed GPU fleet (A100 and older T4s); using naive batching. Unit cost is high, and cold-starts hurt SLAs.
- Requirements called out repeatedly on calls: VPC/private control plane, KMS integration, audit logs with 1y retention, and EU data residency for certain accounts.
- Interest areas with Redwood: intelligent dynamic batching with latency SLOs, prefix/KV caching for repeated route prompts, per-model quantization profiles (4-bit/8-bit) to reduce cost on Dedicated pools, and Redwood Optimize recommendations for batching/caching/quantization knobs.
- Quote from CTO (02/26 call): "If Redwood can shave 20–30% off per-token cost while keeping p95 under 120ms we can move committed capacity."
- POC plan discussed: 2-week controlled benchmark: baseline (current infra) vs Redwood Dedicated (pinned model + quantized variant) with synthetic and sampled production traces. Include cold/warm cache scenarios.
- Security: completed initial questionnaire; next is architecture review with security team (drive: security_faq + encryption_design).
- Logistics: AE/Maya to introduce procurement team in next meeting; SE will scope benchmarking test cases.
- Helpful artifacts: pricing_deck_v2 (drive), security_faq_0301.pdf (drive), transcript Fireflies ff_20260215_9b3f2c.
- Short-hand CRM fragments: POC start target: 2026-03-10; "must hit p95 <= 120ms" reiterated; budget guardrails: prefer monthly committed pricing or reserved Dedicated for peak blocks.

title: Ceres Pronto Inference
preview:
Account background: Ceres Pronto is a payments marketplace (mid-market fintech) building a real-time offer engine and conversational payment assistant. Heavy sensitivity to tail latency; most revenue-critical flows run at <100ms p95 in production. They currently run a mix of CPU microservices and lightweight GPU endpoints in-house.

Primary asks: 
- p95 latency <= 60ms for short-chat + reranking path (prompt length ~80 tokens).
- Sustained throughput target: 100–250 QPS during peak, spiky bursts to 1k QPS expected.

title: Copperstar DataWorks
preview:
Summary:
- Mid-market product analytics SaaS. B2B product with heavy in-app chat + semantic search + personalization reranker.
- Interested in hosted API for getting live quickly, then Dedicated reserved GPU once traffic is steady.

Recent timeline (bulleted):
- 2026-01-28: Intro call (AE Maya Chen) — product goals + metrics. "We need predictable latency for live support and cheaper embeddings for index ops."
- 2026-02-10: Technical deep-dive w/ SE (Ethan) — shared sample traffic profile, token distribution, avg seq len ~120 tokens.
- 2026-02-25: Pricing ask — engineering wants cost per 1M tokens and Dedicated reserved options.
- 2026-03-01: Cost optimization workshop (1.5h) run by Redwood Optimize team. Covered batching, prefix/KV caching, and quantization tradeoffs. Notes captured in Drive doc.
- 2026-03-03: Internal Copperstar infra assessment — preference for VPC + KMS; EU residency for 10% of customers required.

POC / Evaluation scope:
- 2-week POC on Hosted API followed by short Dedicated pilot.
- Goals: achieve avg chat latency <200ms p95, embedding index cost reduction >=35% vs baseline, and reranking throughput 3x baseline.
- POC plan: enable prefix caching for common onboarding flows, tune batching window for 50-150ms, test int8 quantized model variant for cost/latency tradeoff.

Cost workshop highlights (actionable):
- Batching: recommended dynamic batching with 80ms window for chat peak hours — modeling shows ~22% token cost reduction while keeping p95 ~210ms (will push for further kernel optimizations).
- Caching: prefix caching on onboarding flows could remove 12-18% of token usage; need to define cache invalidation policies for privacy-sensitive fields.
- Quantization profiles: try 4-bit quantization on reranker model to cut inference GPU memory by ~40% and reduce per-token cost; signal-check to ensure negligible quality drift on top-10 rerank.
- Unit economics: modeled with Copperstar's traffic (350k monthly active users): combined optimizations project 30-45% lower monthly spend; break-even on Dedicated reserved capacity expected at ~$12k/month committed.

Security / compliance notes:
- Copperstar requires SSO (SAML) + SOC2 and audit logging for initial contract. KMS integration for key management mandatory for Dedicated.
- EU data residency: need private routing for EU subset or Redwood hosted region guarantee.

Quotes / call snippets:
- "If we can get predictable p95 and lower embedding cost, we can justify moving to Dedicated." — Head of Product
- "Show me actual unit economics with our traffic, not generic numbers." — CFO (finance wants POC cost model).

Open items / blockers (short):
- Security team needs architecture diagram + pen-test evidence (SEC-2098).
- Legal needs standard DPA + data residency annex.
- Finance to confirm commit level for Dedicated pilot.

Next steps (detailed):
- 2026-03-05: Deliver POC runbook + cost model (AE/SE).
- 2026-03-10: Workshop w/ infra + product to run optimization tuning; include Redwood Optimize engineer.
- 2026-03-17: Start Hosted API POC (2 weeks).
- After POC: analyze telemetry (tokens broken down by route) and propose Dedicated commit sizing + pricing tier.

Notes style: CRM shorthand, action-items, links to artifacts above.

preview: Status: STALLED — POC not started. title: Deepwell Media Indexing
preview:
Summary:
- Deepwell runs a 20M-document media archive (news articles, transcripts, image captions). avg doc length ~5k words; many very long transcripts.
- Primary need: fast, high-quality search for editors and legal teams (latency target: <300ms to first relevant for UX; 95th <800ms including reranker).
- Cost constraint: want cost/query ~ <$0.02 overall for typical sessions (initial target $0.008 - $0.015 per query), high sensitivity to per-token + reranker GPU time.

Technical / POC plan (from AE/SE call):
- Retrieval stack: dense bi-encoder for candidate retrieval (FAISS) + GPU cross-encoder reranker for top-K. K in discussion (50-200).
- Indexing scale: expect ~80M chunks after chunking (current estimate). Concerned about index sharding, RAM footprint, and search tail latency.
- Chunking experiments: compare 512-token chunks vs 1024-token chunks + 20% overlap. Tradeoffs discussed: larger chunks reduce number of vectors (cost) but increase noisy context for embedding; smaller chunks increase recall for long queries but increases storage and retrieval cost.
- Embeddings: prefer an open embedding model with 1536-d vectors. SE suggested test with sentence-transformers/all-mpnet-base-v2 and a newer open 1536-d candidate in Redwood catalog.
- Reranker tuning: start with a distilled cross-encoder (DistilBERT cross-encoder) for latency; evaluate accuracy uplift vs a stronger BERT-large cross-encoder. Discussed using model temperature/soft-label distillation and calibrating score thresholds to reduce reranker calls (only rerank top-40 most likely).
- Caching & batching: plan to test prefix and query-level caching to reduce calls for repeat queries; dynamic batching for reranker to amortize GPU time.

Security/compliance notes:
- Must be VPC-hosted; legal requires audit logs + KMS integration. Data residency: US-only for now. Security team will run Redwood security questionnaire next week.

Interactions & timeline:
- 2026-01-10: Intro call (AE+SE) — collected high-level requirements.
- 2026-01-28: Deep technical working session; shared sample dataset (anonymized).
- 2026-02-12: POC kickoff call (Fireflies FF-2026-02-12-4582). Agreed on 3-phase POC: ingest + chunk test -> retrieval+rerank -> perf/cost tuning.
- 2026-02-26: Security scoping call (Fireflies FF-2026-02-26-4729).
- 2026-03-01: Engineering sync; Redwood provided cost model (see drive doc).

Quotes / call snippets:
- "If we can't get first-relevant under 300ms the editors won't adopt it" — Head of Search.
- "We need a clear cost per query at 1M queries/month scale" — VP Product.

Open action items / POC success criteria:
- Demonstrate indexing cadence: can ingest 5M docs/day and keep vector index updated with <2hr lag.
- Latency: median to first relevant <300ms, 95th <800ms in Dedicated environment.
- Accuracy: reranker increases eventual MAP@10 by >8% over bi-encoder only baseline.
- Cost: target average infra cost per search session <$0.02 (including retrieval + rerank).

Risks:
- Index growth (80M chunks) increases storage and retrieval costs above estimate. Need to test chunk consolidation heuristics (document-level anchors, metadata priors).
- Reranker GPU cost could dominate if top-K is large; SE suggests top-40 gating + score-thresholding.

Next steps (short):
- Schedule Dedicated canary POC week of 2026-03-15. SE to prepare ingestion script + sample index.
- Legal to push back SSO/SAML details; AE to provide encrypted SAML metadata.
- Redwood to run initial chunk-size A/B on 200k docs and share cost/latency matrix.

CRM style notes (short/fragments):
- Decision maker: VP Product (Samira Hale).
- Procurement lead: Finance (RFP pending).
- Wants reference from similar publisher — ask BD for reference intro.
- Healthy urgency: pilot by end of Q2 2026.

title: BeaconBridge Analytics
preview:
Summary: Mid-market analytics SaaS building an in-app conversational assistant for product support and guided analytics. Primary interest in Hosted API to accelerate time-to-value; evaluating Dedicated for predictable throughput if cost/latency tradeoffs justify it.

Recent discussions: 
- "Initial discovery 2025-11-05": product + infra overview. They emphasized low engineering headcount and desire for a managed endpoint.
- "Demo 2026-01-14": hosted API streaming demo (tool calling + structured outputs). Product team liked streaming but asked about latency p95 under load.
- "POC kickoff 2026-02-01": 2-week POC (hosted) to validate latency SLOs, tool calling flow (external ticketing + DB connector), and log redaction.
- "Security questionnaire delivered 2026-02-12": they returned with follow-ups on SSO, SOC2 evidence, retention windows, and log access.
- "POC interim review 2026-02-24": observed avg first-token latency ~120ms p50, p95 ~380ms under synthetic 40 concurrent chats — above their target p95 300ms. SE adjusted batching and routing; retesting.
- "Pricing intro 2026-03-01": early pricing options shared; customer requested Dedicated sizing estimate vs hosted unit cost at 40-80 concurrent sessions.

Requirements / priorities (customer language):
- "Latency first": aiming for p95 <= 300ms for streaming first token in core agent flow. Must preserve tool call latency (<500ms end-to-end) for ticket creation.
- "Managed first, private later": test Hosted API now, open to Dedicated in 6-12 months if cost predictable.
- "Security must-haves": SSO/SAML integration (Okta), SOC2 Type II evidence, audit logs with PII redaction, ability to set retention policy 30/90/365 days, KMS integration for secrets.
- "Model / quality": prefer open chat models (fine-tuned Llama2-chat family) with automatic fallback to quantized variant for cost/concurrency. Need deterministic tool-calling reliability.

POC success criteria (agreed):
- Achieve p95 streaming first-token <= 300ms for 40 concurrent chat sessions (synthetic load).
- Demonstrate tool-calling reliability >= 99% for ticket create workflow under POC load.
- Security signoff on hosted data flow, DPA, and proof of log PII redaction (redaction scripts or pipeline).

Quotes / paraphrases: 
- "We don't want to own model infra yet — get us running in 2 weeks." — Head of Product
- "If hosted can meet latency reliably, we'll delay Dedicated but need clear route to it." — CTO

Open items: 
- Redwood to provide detailed DPA + network diagram for hosted API (security asked for flow to third-party monitoring).
- SE to run tuned config: reduce batch windows, KV cache priming, and test smaller model variant fallback.
- Finance ask: 12-month cost comparison Hosted vs Dedicated for 40/80 concurrent sustained sessions.

Where we stand: POC mid-progress, security review active, performance tuning in next sprint. High likelihood to convert if latency SLO and redaction proof satisfied.

title: Lilypad Socket
preview:
Summary: inbound SMB trial from organic signup (marketing blog link). Signed up for Hosted API; created first API key but only ~5 calls total over 10 days. Appears to be evaluating multiple vendors — CTO indicated they want 'lowest latency for chat widgets' and are attracted to competitor promo credits. Pricing confused team; misc. console errors they saw may be due to using wrong region. Wants SOC2 + SSO before committing but initial interest is self-serve Hosted API (public cloud).

Recent timeline (short):
- 2025-12-09: created account + free credits auto-provisioned.
- 2025-12-10: first API key created; 2 test completions recorded.
- 2025-12-15: automated onboarding email opened, no follow-up.
- 2026-01-20: AE outreach (intro) — got brief reply from CTO: 'evaluating latency & cost; using competitor credits for PoC'.
- 2026-02-18: 30m demo scheduled; Fireflies ff-2026-02-18-9912. Demo covered streaming & rate limits; team asked about region routing.
- 2026-02-25: trial credits showing low consumption; ticket RDW-4219 opened about perceived 429s on burst tests. Support confirmed it was client side polyfill — not platform.
- 2026-03-01: follow-up email from AE, no response. Last activity: small test request.

Use-case & requirements (from calls):
- Primary: chat widget for customer support (concurrent users 20-40 peak). Need sub-500ms p95 ideally for short queries; willing to accept higher for long-form.
- Secondary: product recommendation reranking for search results (throughput moderate, latency tolerant).
- Must be SOC2-level controls, SSO (SAML) and audit logging for compliance with vendor policy. Data residency not strict — US cloud OK.
- Cost sensitivity: SMB budget; looking for predictable token economics and clear burst behavior.

Observed risks / why trial is at-risk:

title: CodeLattice Labs
preview:
Short summary: mid-stage IDE copilots vendor building embedded code assistance for enterprise dev teams. Primary ask: low-latency streaming and deterministic UX in editors (VS Code + JetBrains). TTFT focus — want p50 <= 80ms, jitter minimized, smooth token streaming (no stuttering). Token budget policy: per-session token cap + adaptive cutoff for long completions. PoC constraints: integrate with hosted API first; must show model pinning + automatic fallback (smaller quantized model) under load.

Recent call notes (fragmented CRM style):
- "When autocomplete stutters devs lose context" — product lead quote.
- Concern: websocket disconnects cause duplicate suggestions; want idempotent deltas and resume token offsets.
- Prototype uses continuous streaming (SSE) with 16-token chunking, but jitter at client seen when GC spikes on local plugin.
- Backend asks: support pinned model per-user and per-repo; ability to route to dedicated instances for enterprise customers.
- Cost sensitivity: willing to pay more for <100ms TTFT for premium users; default users ok with 200-300ms.
- Security: must support SSO (SAML), corporate KMS, audit logs, and per-repo data residency notes.

POC details and acceptance criteria (short):
- TTFT p50 <= 80ms across 95% of sessions in EU/NA regions.
- No visible token burst > 3x after first token (jitter threshold).
- Backpressure policy ensures editor keeps first-token latency even under 100 concurrent dev sessions per org.
- Model variant fallback: if dedicated capacity saturated, auto-route to pinned quantized variant with failover TTL 30s.

Action items:
- Diego to run 1-hour stress run (50 VS Code clients) w/ pinned model and report p50/p95/p99.
- Priya to circulate security FAQ and KMS integration checklist.
- Legal to review KMS SOW.

CRM shorthand:
- next: demo w/ token-budget tuning + canary pinning (Ava)

title: Calypso ServeFlow
preview:
Summary: Calypso ServeFlow is a fintech payments orchestration platform that wants to embed real-time conversational and recommendation features into their merchant dashboard. Very latency- and cost-sensitive — they price UX per-transaction and need predictable unit economics.

Key constraints / asks (from CTO + infra lead):
- SLO targets: median latency <= 35-50ms for 128-256 token chat paths; p95 < 75-90ms.
- Throughput: baseline 200 qps with bursts to 1k qps during peak settlement windows.
- Cost sensitivity: target incremental cost <= $0.50 per 1000 messages (after caching/batching).
- Model preferences: open Llama-style models for deterministic licensing; willing to evaluate small proprietary variants if latency improves.
- Routing/fallback: need policy for model fallback to lower-cost quantized variants during capacity pressure.
- Security: VPC + customer-managed KMS, audit logs retained 1 year, SSO/SAML for internal users, and EU data residency for some merchant data.

Optimization interest: batching (tunable max batch latency tradeoffs), KV/prefix caching for repeated prompts (merchant-specific templates), and FP16/8-bit quantization profiles. CTO asked: "Can you get similar quality at 4-bit quantized + dynamic dequant on GPU?" SE notes: test quantized 4/8-bit + Redwood Optimize recommendations.

Perf bench ask (explicit): produce benchmark matrix that varies: model variant (float16, 8-bit, 4-bit), batch window (1ms, 5ms, 20ms), cache hit rate (0%, 30%, 60%), sequence lengths (avg 120, 256, 512). Measure median/p95/p99 latency, throughput, tokens/sec, and cost per 1M tokens. Provide kernel/profile traces for hotspots.

POC results so far (notes):
- Baseline hosted API (float16, no cache): median ~62ms, p95 ~110ms at 200 qps.
- Experimental Dedicated with batching=5ms and KV cache (~40% hit): median ~38ms, p95 ~70ms, cost down ~37%.
- 4-bit quantization reduced GPU memory by ~2x; quality regression flagged on domain-specific prompts (fraud detection phrases).

Quotes from calls:
- CTO: "If we can hit p95 <80ms and cut incremental token cost in half, this is a no-brainer."
- PM: "We need a clear rollback plan — can't compromise fraud detection model outputs."

Next steps (detailed):
- Redwood to run the perf bench with traffic replay (bench due 2026-03-08).
- SE to deliver config suggestions: batch windows, cache TTLs, quantization profile mapping per model family.
- Security to provide SOC2 artifacts and KMS integration guide; Calypso to run internal pen-test.
- Schedule procurement review with finance after bench (forecast May close).

Administrative: pricing deck in Drive, security Q&A in Drive, open Jira RED-7891 for quantization quality regression on fraud prompts.

title: Acorn Financial
preview:
Account summary:
- Large enterprise financial services org. Evaluating Redwood for a mix of internal assistant + customer support summarization + doc search. Strong preference for VPC/Private deployment (hosted acceptable for early evaluation only).

Key requirements (why this deal is in the SDK parity path):
- Streaming reliability is a must-have. They see frequent mid-stream disconnects behind corporate proxies and want predictable client behavior:
  - Clear surfaced error when the stream breaks (include request_id and partial output context).
  - Guidance on what is safe to retry vs not safe to retry ("we cannot double-send an action" / avoid duplicate side effects).
  - Prefer that SDKs do NOT automatically retry mid-stream; instead expose enough metadata for app-level retries w/ idempotency.
- Strict timeout expectations:
  - They enforce hard end-to-end request budgets at their gateway (customer cited: "we kill requests at 15s unless explicitly approved").
  - Want consistent timeout knobs across Python/TypeScript/Go, including a clear overall timeout model and sane defaults.
  - Past pain point: Python streaming appeared to ignore configured timeouts (they referenced similar behavior to SUP-29505 symptoms).
- Retry behavior expectations:
  - Must respect Retry-After on 429 and back off aggressively to avoid triggering internal incident response (they have alerting on client retry storms).
  - Retryable set must be conservative and consistent (no retries on 4xx like 400/401/403). They asked for a written list of retryable codes + network failures.
  - Request_id must be present in all errors for triage (they have a runbook requiring it).

Deployment + security notes:
- Deployment: VPC preferred; private networking and KMS integration required. Data residency likely US-only for initial workloads.
- Security checklist items repeatedly mentioned:
  - SSO/SAML for console access; audit logs; retention controls; log redaction guarantees.
  - SDK auth handling: explicit parameter > env vars; consistent env var names; do not log secrets even in debug. They want this in writing.

Artifacts shared / referenced:
- Security/auth guidance shared: sources/confluence/security-and-compliance/rbac-and-auth/sdk-auth-resolution-and-secret-handling.json
- SDK migration notes shared: sources/confluence/eng-developer-experience/docs-site/sdk-migration-notes-auth-retry-timeout.json
- Customer impact brief (PM-facing but used to explain rollout): sources/google_drive/shared_drives/product/hosted-api/sdk-standardization-customer-impact-brief.json
- Fireflies references:
  - 2026-01-23-redwood-technical-deep-dive-sdk-retries-and-streaming
  - 2026-02-12-security-review-auth-handling-in-sdks

Open questions / follow-ups:
- Confirm their required timeout budget for streaming vs non-streaming calls (do they allow longer for streaming?).
- Validate behavior behind their proxy with the beta SDKs (focus: Retry-After on 429, stream disconnect error shape, request_id propagation).
- Provide a short recommended config snippet per language (Python/TS/Go) that matches the new standard and includes logging fields: request_id, retry_count, timeout_ms, streaming_disconnect_reason.

Internal:
- This account is a driver for the parity initiative success criteria (support volume + confidence in reliability). Keep them on the beta list once SDK releases are ready; coordinate with Ibrahim Khan (SDK parity TL) for any last-mile issues and with Marcus Lin for security signoff language.

title: Cobalt Orbit Inference
preview:
Background:
- Mid-size logistics platform, operates real-time route optimization + driver assistant chat. High-read consistency for embeddings + sub-100ms soft SLO for short chat turns on edge routes.
- Interested in Redwood Dedicated for predictable throughput and data isolation; on-prem option for EU-sensitive customers.

Primary objectives (from AE/SE discussions):
- Throughput guarantees for business hours regional peaks: target 12k RPS of small-chat tokens across 3 regions (NA/EU/APAC) with 99th percentile latency SLOs: 50-120ms depending on model variant.
- Predictable reserved GPU planning: want recommendation for reserved vs burst mix (monthly commit + burst on demand). Need example ARRs for 4x/6x utilization scenarios.
- Reliability: cross-region failover with <5s cold-swap for routing + model-variant fallback when primary variants saturate. Documented sequence for model fallbacks and token-level impact.
- SLA negotiation points: explicit MTTI for failover, credit matrix for SLO breaches, and runbook sharing for DR tests.

Technical constraints / preferences:
- Favor open/quantized model variants for cost (int8/int4) but require quality gates.
- KV cache warmup time critical for long-session chat — want plan for pre-warmed caches across dedicated pools.
- Network egress restrictions for EU customers -> must support customer-managed VPC + private endpoints.
- Must support SAML SSO + audit log forwarding to SIEM.

POC design agreed (high level):
- Phase 1 (2 weeks): Benchmarks with synthetic replay of 72-hour peak pattern. Measure p50/p95/p99 per-model and token cost.
- Phase 2 (3 weeks): Failover drills across NA->EU and EU->APAC; validate routing TTLs, DNS vs anycast tradeoffs, and latency spike behavior.
- Phase 3 (2 weeks): Model-variant saturation tests: define 3 variants (high-accuracy, balanced, low-cost quantized) and exercise automatic fallback policy while recording quality metrics.

Key questions we raised for Redwood team:
- Can Dedicated expose metric streams for per-pool cache hit rates and KV cache warm-up? (SE: yes, via console + tracing hooks).
- How are reserved GPU SKUs billed when autoscaling triggers burst capacity? Need sample invoice.
- Can we get a templated SLA addendum that includes cross-region failover RTO/RPO and credits?

Quotes / paraphrases from customer calls:
- \"If failover takes longer than the billing cycle, we lose SLAs with our fleet customers.\"
- \"We need to know exact behavior when the primary model is saturated — do you queue, reject, or downgrade model?\"

Action items (AE/SE):
- Jordan: circulate Dedicated sizing spreadsheet with 3 plan options (conservative / baseline / aggressive) by 2026-02-24 (done).
- Priya (SE): provide synthetic load scripts and initial benchmark harness; propose cache warm-up pattern by 2026-02-28.
- Marcus (CSM): draft SLA addendum template + examples of credits for p99 breaches by 2026-03-03.

Open risks / notes to legal/procurement:
- Customer will push for a shorter penalty window for failover SLA (30m) vs Redwood standard (2h). Expect negotiation.
- Data residency: some fleets require EU-only model inference. Need clear routing controls and regional isolation docs.

Stubs / references:
- POC benchmark plan: drive.google.com/open?id=cobalt-poc-bench-plan
- Security FAQ reviewed: drive.google.com/open?id=cobalt-security-faq
- Transcript: ff-2026-02-14-7892 (contains CSO Q&A on KMS + key rotation)

Short next-step summary:
- Redwood to deliver dedicated SKU proposal + failover runbook draft. Legal to exchange SLA addendum. POC kickoff when SOW signed.

- Network egress restrictions for EU customers -> must support customer-managed VPC + private endpoints.
- Must support SAML SSO + audit log forwarding to SIEM.

POC design agreed (high level):
- Phase 1 (2 weeks): Benchmarks with synthetic replay of 72-hour peak pattern. Measure p50/p95/p99 per-model and token cost.
- Phase 2 (3 weeks): Failover drills across NA->EU and EU->APAC; validate routing TTLs, DNS vs anycast tradeoffs, and latency spike behavior.
- Phase 3 (2 weeks): Model-variant saturation tests: define 3 variants (high-accuracy, balanced, low-cost quantized) and exercise automatic fallback policy while recording quality metrics.

Key questions we raised for Redwood team:
- Can Dedicated expose metric streams for per-pool cache hit rates and KV cache warm-up? (SE: yes, via console + tracing hooks).
- How are reserved GPU SKUs billed when autoscaling triggers burst capacity? Need sample invoice.
- Can we get a templated SLA addendum that includes cross-region failover RTO/RPO and credits?

Quotes / paraphrases from customer calls:
- \"If failover takes longer than the billing cycle, we lose SLAs with our fleet customers.\"
- \"We need to know exact behavior when the primary model is saturated — do you queue, reject, or downgrade model?\"

Action items (AE/SE):
- Jordan: circulate Dedicated sizing spreadsheet with 3 plan options (conservative / baseline / aggressive) by 2026-02-24 (done).
- Priya (SE): provide synthetic load scripts and initial benchmark harness; propose cache warm-up pattern by 2026-02-28.
- Marcus (CSM): draft SLA addendum template + examples of credits for p99 breaches by 2026-03-03.

Open risks / notes to legal/procurement:
- Customer will push for a shorter penalty window for failover SLA (30m) vs Redwood standard (2h). Expect negotiation.
- Data residency: some fleets require EU-only model inference. Need clear routing controls and regional isolation docs.

Stubs / references:
- POC benchmark plan: drive.google.com/open?id=cobalt-poc-bench-plan
- Security FAQ reviewed: drive.google.com/open?id=cobalt-security-faq
- Transcript: ff-2026-02-14-7892 (contains CSO Q&A on KMS + key rotation)

Short next-step summary:
- Redwood to deliver dedicated SKU proposal + failover runbook draft. Legal to exchange SLA addendum. POC kickoff when SOW signed.

title: Aquifer Autonomous Insights
preview:
Lead: Jordan Blake (AE). SE: Priya Menon. CSM: Alex Ruiz assigned for transition planning.

Discovery summary:
- 2025-10-07: Intro call; Aquifer uses LLMs for vehicle incident triage + operator chat. Interested in dedicated GPU pools for deterministic latency.
- 2025-11-12: Shared annotated prompt set (2.1k examples) and initial traffic profile (peak 600 rps for embeddings; 120 rps chat during surge).
- 2025-12-02: Security questionnaire submitted; SOC2 + KMS required. Data residency: US only for PII subsets.
- 2026-01-18: Architecture workshop with SE team; discussed KV-cache, batching, and multi-model routing for cost control.
- 2026-02-10: POC kickoff call (Fireflies id ff_20260210_aurora-poc-call). Agreed test plan and metrics.

POC goals (what Aquifer needs):
- Validate sustained throughput on Dedicated pool: 3k tokens/sec sustained with 99% of requests meeting latency SLO.
- Tail latency SLOs: p95 < 120ms for 64-token responses; p99 < 350ms for 128-token responses.
- Cost profile: unit token cost target to be 30-40% below current cloud-hosted baseline using batching + prefix cache.
- Reliability: 99.9% uptime SLA, cross-region fallback during planned maintenance.

POC test plan (high level):
- Load tests: synthetic traffic generated from k6 sim with replayed request shapes (chat short, chat long, embeddings, rerank). Target ramps to peak load observed in prod profile.
- Soak tests: 72-hour run at 70% of peak reserved capacity to surface memory leaks, KV cache saturation, and autoscale behavior.
- Representative prompts: 2.1k annotated prompts plus 1k support transcripts for safety/regression checks.
- Metrics captured: p50/p95/p99 latency, tokens/sec, GPU utilization per instance, batch size distribution, error/retry rate, cost per 1M tokens.
- Quality gates: BLEU/similarity and a small eval harness to detect regressions vs baseline open model.

Technical/Capacity notes:
- Aquifer expects to reserve capacity equivalent to 8xA100-80GB (or equivalent q4/8 quantized V100 class) for initial staging; will iterate after POC.
- Prefers model routing policies: primary high-accuracy variant during business hours, budget variant for batch jobs.
- KMS integration required before production cutover; security team flagged HSM requirement for keys used to unwrap telemetry.

Negotiation/Commercial items in play:
- SLA: Aquifer pushing for 99.95% with financial credits; Redwood proposed 99.9% for Dedicated baseline and escalation path.
- Pricing: committed capacity discount tiers; Aquifer requests committed-month-to-month flexibility for first 3 months of POC-to-prod ramp.
- Term: procurement evaluating 12–24 month reservation options.

Recent activity and decisions:
- 2026-02-12: Runbook and JMeter scripts received from Aquifer infra. SE finalized native k6 test harness.
- 2026-02-14: Security sent SOC2 report; awaiting KMS attestation.
- 2026-02-15: Dry run on a small Dedicated staging pool to validate tooling and metrics capture. p95 for 64-token responses ~110ms, p99 ~320ms — promising.

Open items / Blockers (context):
- Legal redlines: Aquifer legal added HSM + data residency clauses that require review (ticket SEC-4104).
- On-prem option: Aquifer evaluating air-gapped subset; procurement timeline for on-prem infra is 8-12 weeks which would push production start.
- KMS path: need confirmation whether customer will use cloud KMS or bring their own HSM; affects rollout schedule.

Next steps (operational):
- Week of 2026-02-20: Start 72-hour soak at agreed 70% capacity on Dedicated staging pool. SE to monitor and feed real-time dashboards to Aquifer SREs.
- 2026-03-04: Review soak results, adjust reserved GPU sizing and batching configs.
- 2026-03-10: Legal and Redwood finalize SLA redlines; commercial to issue committed capacity quote for 12 months.

Sales notes (short):
- High strategic: potential 500k-1M ARR first year if SLA + KMS cleared.
- Sensitive to unit cost; Optimize team to run quantization and batching recommendations during POC.
- Tone: pragmatic, engineering-led buying group.

Quoted lines: "We need predictable tail latency during surge windows — interruptions equal customer downtime for operators," - head of platform (paraphrase).

title: ClarityMesh
preview:
Background: ClarityMesh is a mid-market SaaS vendor that powers searchable knowledge bases for B2B SaaS customers (support docs, onboarding guides, internal KB). They operate multi-tenant SaaS with a high read volume on KB search and expect peak QPS for search reranking up to ~500 qps across regions.

Primary ask: Hosted embeddings + reranking pipeline. Need low-latency top-K retrieval and a tuned reranker to push relevant KB passages to the assistant. Looking for predictable per-query economics at scale — current internal run-rate on their homegrown vector stack is getting expensive in CPU/GPU ops, want to offload to Redwood if cost/latency wins.

Indexing profile: ~12M total passages today (regularly deduped), incremental ingest cadence: hourly deltas (~10k docs/hr), retention for support docs 3+ years. Currently use 1536-d embeddings, exploring 1024 quantized models for cost. Want support for near-real-time partial re-indexing and soft deletes.

Performance targets (customer stated):
- 95th pctile end-to-end query latency (retrieve + rerank) < 200ms for single-region queries (NA) under steady traffic.
- Peak throughput: sustained 500 qps, burst 1k qps for 2–5 min windows.
- Cost target: effective per-query cost (embeddings+rerank+storage) <= $0.0007 for baseline workload.

Model & routing preferences: Prefer open models for rerank (fine-tuned lightweight re-ranker) and lower-cost embedding models for vector index. Wants automatic fallback to cheaper model variant on capacity events; route read-heavy traffic to cached responses and tiered model pools.

Security/compliance: Must support SSO (SAML), audit logs for admin actions, KMS integration for key management, and EU data residency for a segment of customers. SOC2 Type II is a firm blocker toward procurement.

POC plan discussed: 2-week hosted API POC. Scope: replicate current traffic profile with synthetic replay (daily cadence + bursts), onboard a subset of 2M docs, run A/B against current in-house stack for latency/cost and relevance metrics (MRR@10, NDCG). Success criteria: <=200ms p95, relevance >= baseline, per-query cost meets target.

Recent activity timeline (CRM notes):
- 2026-01-20: Initial inbound via pricing form; AE assigned (Ava).
- 2026-01-22: Intro call (30m) — product fit confirmed, asked for hosted API latency numbers. (ff_20260122_introcall)
- 2026-01-28: Deep dive with SE (Marco) on indexing pattern and reranker architecture; shared sample dataset and synthetic QPS profile. Fireflies: ff_20260128_clarity-poc-call.
- 2026-02-03: Sent draft POC proposal + pricing assumptions (drive link). Waiting on legal/compliance to provide SOC2 evidence.
- 2026-02-10: Follow-up demo of streaming embeddings ingestion; discussed caching + KV prefix reuse for users with repeated queries.

Sales notes / quotes from calls: "We need a provider that behaves like a CDN for our knowledge—fast, cheap, and predictable." — Head of Search.

Technical notes / SE observations: Reranker latency is the critical path; prefer running reranker on CPU-optimized instance or small GPU that supports batched scoring. Embedding compute dominates cost at high QPS unless caching or dimensionality reduction applied. Recommend Redwood Optimize suggestions: 1) 1024-d quantized embeddings + productized reranker model, 2) aggressive prefix caching for sessionized users, 3) dynamic routing to cheaper model pool during non-peak windows.

Next steps (detailed):
- Legal to confirm SOC2 docs by 2026-02-18 (blocked).
- SE to provision hosted API POC environment + sample index (Marco) — ETA 2 business days after SOC2 confirmation.
- Run synthetic 72-hr stress replay with 500 qps steady and 1k qps bursts during POC; collect p95 latencies and token costs.
- Deliver POC report with cost projection and recommended config (batching, cache TTLs, quantization profile).

Short term ask from AE: "If we can show p95 <200ms and cost delta >= 25% vs their infra, we convert to paid Dedicated in trial 6–9 months."

title: Devflow AI
preview:
HubSpot Company Record — Devflow AI (devflow.ai)

Summary
- Mid-market devtools team building an LLM-powered coding assistant inside their IDE + web app.
- Came in via self-serve Hosted API; now in evaluation with potential Dedicated capacity request if p95 latency holds at launch traffic.
- Strong sensitivity to onboarding friction (they’re modeling their own user onboarding after ours).

Lifecycle touchpoints (sprint instrumentation)
- Onboarding Email v2: received (Variant B; subject emphasized “fast first request”).
  - Observed behavior: clicked CTA → quickstart page → returned to console within ~6 minutes.
  - Feedback: “The email got us to the right page, but the snippet didn’t match our setup (we’re TS/Node + Vite).”
- Console nudges:
  - Nudge shown: Create API key (completed).
  - Nudge shown: Make your first request (clicked; led to docs). Dismissal persisted correctly after completion (no repeat complaint).
  - Nudge shown: Enable streaming (they asked what it changes for TTFT; wants best-practice defaults).

Activation / product usage notes
- Activation milestones (qualitative from call + event notes):
  - First successful request: achieved after initial 401 error (likely copied wrong env var / key mismatch).
  - Time-to-first-token: “Felt fine locally” but wants measured p95 under concurrency.
  - First 100k tokens: not yet at time of call; they were still prototyping prompt formats.
- SDK notes:
  - Using TypeScript SDK.
  - Early error handling: they hit “missing/invalid API key” path; asked for clearer guidance in-console on where the key is expected (env var name + example).

Evaluation requirements (from 2026-01-22 call)
- Workload
  - Interactive chat/code completion; many short prompts, frequent streaming.
  - Potential embeddings for repo/doc indexing (not immediate).
- Performance targets
  - Wants “snappy” streaming start; specifically asked to minimize perceived delay at response start.
  - Interested in batching/caching guidance later (Optimize mentioned as follow-on).
- Reliability / ops
  - Wants predictable behavior during capacity contention; asked about fallback behavior and how errors present to the client.
- Security/compliance
  - SOC 2 requested.
  - Asked about audit logs and retention controls (they sometimes pass code snippets from customers).
  - SSO/SAML: “nice to have” now, may become required if they roll to larger enterprise accounts.

Activation blockers / friction points
- 401 on first request (copy/paste / env var mismatch). They described it as: “We weren’t sure if it was the key, the model name, or our network.”
  - Suggested mitigation: tighten first-request snippet + highlight the exact env var name; in-console status check for ‘key seen’ would help.
- Docs mismatch for their stack (TS/Node). They want a minimal snippet that matches the SDK they installed.
- Confusion around streaming defaults:
  - They asked whether they should always stream, and what settings are recommended to optimize TTFT vs throughput.
- Email deliverability: no issue reported by Devflow AI directly, but note: their domain has strict filtering; keep an eye if future lifecycle sends bounce/complain.

Stakeholder notes
- Champion: Head of Product Engineering (name redacted in CRM export); very hands-on.
- Technical evaluator: platform engineer who owns their inference layer; wants a short ‘load-test recipe’.

Internal follow-ups / links
- Fireflies transcript: fireflies/sales-calls/2026-01-22-onboarding-feedback-devflow-ai
- Related lifecycle experiment context: lifecycle growth sprint launch brief (ben_carter/20260127-launch-brief-lifecycle-growth-experiments)
- Messaging consistency thread used for copy alignment: soojin_lee/20260118-messaging-review-onboarding-sequence
- Support reference (not necessarily this account, but relevant symptom pattern): SUP-24871 ‘not receiving onboarding emails’

Next steps
- Request a sample prompt distribution + expected concurrency to run a lightweight benchmark (include streaming on/off comparison).
- Send SOC 2 + retention controls summary; confirm if they need SSO/SAML before launch.
- If performance targets are strict, introduce Dedicated option with a small reserved pool for launch month.

Latest AE/SE note (2026-02-07)
- They’re leaning positive on Hosted API for pilot, but want a clear plan for scaling (Dedicated) and guardrails (fallbacks, quotas) before committing to a launch timeline.

  - Interested in batching/caching guidance later (Optimize mentioned as follow-on).
- Reliability / ops
  - Wants predictable behavior during capacity contention; asked about fallback behavior and how errors present to the client.
- Security/compliance
  - SOC 2 requested.
  - Asked about audit logs and retention controls (they sometimes pass code snippets from customers).
  - SSO/SAML: “nice to have” now, may become required if they roll to larger enterprise accounts.

- 401 on first request (copy/paste / env var mismatch). They described it as: “We weren’t sure if it was the key, the model name, or our network.”
  - Suggested mitigation: tighten first-request snippet + highlight the exact env var name; in-console status check for ‘key seen’ would help.
- Docs mismatch for their stack (TS/Node). They want a minimal snippet that matches the SDK they installed.
- Confusion around streaming defaults:
  - They asked whether they should always stream, and what settings are recommended to optimize TTFT vs throughput.
- Email deliverability: no issue reported by Devflow AI directly, but note: their domain has strict filtering; keep an eye if future lifecycle sends bounce/complain.

Stakeholder notes
- Champion: Head of Product Engineering (name redacted in CRM export); very hands-on.
- Technical evaluator: platform engineer who owns their inference layer; wants a short ‘load-test recipe’.

- Request a sample prompt distribution + expected concurrency to run a lightweight benchmark (include streaming on/off comparison).
- Send SOC 2 + retention controls summary; confirm if they need SSO/SAML before launch.
- If performance targets are strict, introduce Dedicated option with a small reserved pool for launch month.

- They’re leaning positive on Hosted API for pilot, but want a clear plan for scaling (Dedicated) and guardrails (fallbacks, quotas) before committing to a launch timeline.

title: Backstop Commerce
preview:
Company overview: Backstop Commerce is a mid-market e-commerce platform focused on post-purchase customer experience (returns, exchanges, shipping updates) across multiple storefronts. Primary users for Redwood are Product/Engineering (chat agents) and Ops (peak capacity planning).

Sales/Eng summary: evaluating Hosted API first to validate latency SLOs for streaming chat + tool-calling, then considering Dedicated for predictable peak season capacity and data residency. Cost-sensitive but willing to commit if Dedicated demonstrates unit cost improvements under high-concurrency workloads.

Key requirements / success metrics:
- p95 latency target: <= 250ms for streaming partials during normal traffic, <= 350ms absolute cap during peak spikes; overall want consistent p95 across customer sessions.
- Peak season scaling: Black Friday/Cyber Monday projected 4-6x baseline concurrency; need autoscale/burst plan and predictable tail-latency.
- Tool calling: agent must call order API, returns system, and shipment tracking webhooks reliably within flow (sync tool calls <500ms preferred).
- Session affinity / KV cache: reuse KV cache across agent sessions to reduce token costs and latency.
- Observability: token-level latency and per-route p95 dashboards, retention for 6 months for audits.

Recent timeline (CRM-style):
- 2025-10-20: inbound interest (product) via website; AE assigned (Jordan).
- 2025-10-27: discovery call (Jordan + Maya) — high-level requirements, demo request.
- 2025-11-03: hosted API demo showing streaming + tool-calls; internal tech lead joined. Demo notes: streaming partials looked snappy; Redwood demo p95 ~180ms for short-turn utterances in controlled env.
- 2025-11-05: technical deep-dive FF call (ff-20251105-7842) — discussed KV caching, fallback model variants, routing by latency tier.
- 2025-11-12: security questionnaire kickoff (ff-20251112-1120) — legal wants SOC2 + ISO details, KMS options, retention controls. Jira SEC-77 opened for encryption questions.
- 2025-11-15: requested hosted POC plan and pricing ranges (see drive POC deck).

Customer quotes / notes from stakeholders:
- "We can't let p95 spikes exceed 350ms during peak — that drives retries and CS load" — Head of Engineering.
- "If hosted proves reliable under our simulated holiday traffic, Dedicated pricing/commit would be straightforward" — Head of Product.

POC scope (agreed):
- 2-week hosted POC: instrument streaming chat + tool-calling against a synthetic load that simulates 5x holiday peak; capture p50/p95/p99 for token streaming, tool-call latencies, and end-to-end completion time.
- Evaluate fallback behavior: model variant routing (cheaper/quantized) when capacity constrained.
- Validate KV caching impact on average token count and latency.

Open questions / blockers:
- Security: legal/infra need SOC2 + ISO docs and KMS architecture diagram (SEC-77).
- Traffic generation: need Redwood-supplied load script or plan to reproduce Black Friday pattern.
- Pricing: prefer committed Dedicated pricing comparison (monthly commit) vs hosted per-token at projected traffic; need model for break-even.

Next steps (short):
- Redwood: deliver hosted POC plan & load script, final POC dates, and Dedicated pricing ranges (Jordan).
- Backstop: schedule security review slot, provide sample order API endpoints for tool-calling POC, and grant test data access behind VPC if needed.

Linked artifacts: drive://Backstop/Redwood/POC-Plan-v1 (POC steps + load profile), drive://Backstop/Redwood/Security-FAQ (SOC2/ISO answers).

Notes formatting: CRM shorthand, expect additional legal questions after POC.

title: Coppergate Inference Works
preview: Workload: synchronous per-request reranking for auction pipeline (95ms target p95), background embeddings for offline features.,Throughput target: sustained 120k tokens/sec across cluster during business hours, ability to scale to 350k tokens/sec for 60-second bursts during peak auctions.,Concurrency caps: prefer soft concurrency limit per-tenant (200 concurrent requests) with queueing for short spikes; hard limit for safety at 600 concurrent requests.,Burst policy: allow burst beyond reserved tokens/sec for up to 90s, billed separately or charged against burst pool; customer requests burst not to cannibalize reserved throughput metrics used for SLA credits.,Latency SLOs: p50 < 40ms, p95 < 95ms, p99 < 200ms for the reranking path when under reserved capacity.,Model prefs: 7B-13B dense models for latency budget; interested in quantized variants if Redwood can guarantee p95 latency at target throughput.,Routing/fallback: automatic fallback to smaller model family or dedicated low-cost pool if reserved capacity saturated; require predictable downgrade behavior and traceability in Console.,Security: SSO (SAML) integration required, customer-managed KMS for keying, SOC2 compliance evidence and audit log retention for 365 days.,Measurement: want tokens/sec measured at request ingress and also validated by Redwood's console (token accounting) with agreed aggregation window (5-minute and 1-hour rolling averages).

title: Borealis SecureAI
preview:
- Initial interest: build IR assistant and internal doc search across incident logs.
- 2024-06-10 intro call: strong exec buy-in (CTO + Head of Product). AO: Jordan Hayes.
- 2024-07-21 demo: showed private deployment flow + latency/cost estimates. Customer asked for on-prem options and KMS integration.
- Security team responded 2024-08: requires full architecture whitepaper, penetration testing plan, and table-top audit before any POC.
- Key quote from Head of Security: "We cannot start a proof-of-concept until we validate offline execution and HSM key control."
- SE (Priya) supplied architecture doc and quantization notes 2024-08-05; security requested longer retention controls and proof of SOC2 evidence.
- Internal champion (Principal ML Engineer, Diego Alvarez) left the company 2024-09; project lost momentum.
- 2024-09-12 finance flagged hiring/budget freeze; procurement deprioritized infra spends until Q2.
- Competitor note: customer ran a pilot with a hyperscaler-managed endpoint (lower friction) during evaluation; leadership preferred turnkey SLA.
- Current status: POC not started — couldn't align on success criteria, security gating and resourcing.
- Suggested re-engage cadence: reconnect in 2025-Q2 after budget cycle; offer a pre-approved security pack and a time-limited evaluation SLA.
- Owner comment: "Good technical fit but organizational timing is the blocker. Will archive and attempt warm re-open later."
